steps.title: Summarize the full structure
steps.content: The passage introduces the silversword alliance as a Hawaiian plant family and gives an example species, Argyroxiphium sandwicense. It then describes the alliance's unusual variety, including vines, shrubs, and trees. Finally, it explains that all members descended from one ancestral tarweed and diversified while adapting to different island habitats.

steps.title: Reject too broad or narrow choices
steps.content:
• Choice A - To advance the claim that all plants on the Hawaiian Islands are part of the silversword alliance and list possible ancestors of the plants that make up the alliance: the text says the alliance grows only in Hawaii, not that all Hawaiian plants belong to it.
  • Choice B - To indicate the specific tarweed ancestor of all plants that grow on the Hawaiian Islands and explain why the plants have such varied physical characteristics: the ancestor is for the alliance only, and no specific tarweed species is named.

steps.title: Choose the full purpose
steps.content:
• Choice C - To describe the silversword alliance and explain how the plant family became so varied: this matches both the description and the diversification explanation.
  • Choice D - To describe the specific habitat where Argyroxiphium sandwicense are found and identify other plants that share a common ancestor with them: no specific habitat is described.
The answer is C.
